extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked on the penultimate Monte Carpegna ascent, soloed to victory with nearly 4 minutes gap over VINGEGAARD and LANDA.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR deliberately exhausted UAE teammates early on Monte Carpegna, signaling pre-planned solo attack rather than late acceleration.
EVENEPOEL hemorrhaged 3:35 on single climb, revealing ciclamino jersey holder vulnerable on sustained gradients above 10%.
LANDA and VINGEGAARD refused to cooperate chasing POGAČAR despite 4-minute deficit, prioritizing podium placement over damage limitation.
SIMMONS waited for CICCONE rather than pressing KOM advantage, suggesting Intermarché prioritized GC over jersey competition.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: launched winning move when team exhausted, showing he plans maximum-difficulty solos when domestique support expires
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: lost 3:35 on single Monte Carpegna ascent while in ciclamino jersey, catastrophic climbing weakness exposed
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: stronger than recent form, second on stage but refused to chase with VINGEGAARD despite massive time loss
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: chastised LANDA for attacking but still lost 4 minutes, suggesting conservative tactics backfired
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: lost vital seconds on descent with sketchy moments, dropped from podium contention by descending weakness
rider_signals.SIMMONS Quinn: neutral: waited for CICCONE instead of pressing KOM jersey advantage, team tactics overrode personal classification
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: neutral: required SIMMONS pacing assistance on climb, not strong enough to contest KOM independently
